ratio_source_quote: 1:1 molar ratio; 608.1 mg IND and 391.6 mg NAP
process_details: Melted in aluminum dishes (volume approx. 100 mL; bottom diameter 8 cm) in a preheated oven at 168 °C for 5 min, quench cooled by pouring liquid nitrogen onto the samples. Sample dishes placed in a desiccator containing phosphorus pentoxide; liquid nitrogen poured into the dishes and allowed to evaporate before IR measurements.
detection_methods: ATR-FTIR; XRPD upon recrystallization showed separation into crystalline single components (no co-crystal).
source_table_or_figure: Section 2.2.1 (Sample preparation); Fig. 5b (co-amorphous NAPIND IR spectrum)
source_quote: Amorphous samples (IND, NAP and a 1:1 molar mixture of both drugs) were prepared by melting them in aluminum dishes ... at 168 °C for 5 min and subsequent quench cooling by pouring liquid nitrogen onto the samples. Before quench cooling of the 1:1 molar ratio, a physical mixture was prepared by gently mixing 1000 mg of the drugs (1:1 molar ratio; 608.1 mg IND and 391.6 mg NAP).
notes: Spectroscopic/computational study (ATR-FTIR + DFT B3-LYP/6-31G(d)); focus on heterodimer formation between NAP and IND in co-amorphous form. No formal stability test in this paper. Tm values from Section 2.1: NAP 158.1 °C (431.25 K), IND γ polymorph 162.0 °C (435.15 K). Reported NAP single-component Tg = 5.04 °C in cited Lobmann et al. 2011, not the mixture Tg.
review_reasons: Stabiiliuskoetta ei raportoitu (vain ATR-FTIR/DFT-tutkimus); stabiilisuusdata viitatussa Lobmann 2011 -artikkelissa (DOI 10.1021/mp2002973)
Mahdollinen duplikaatti Lobmann 2011 -korpuksen NAP+IND 1:1 mol -rivin kanssa; tarkista deduplikointi
